vendor_context: Anker competitor (power banks, charging, cables, portable power stations)
status: ACTIVE DTC — Banned from Amazon since June 2021 (review manipulation). Pivoted to ravpower.com DTC channel. Parent: Sunvalleytek International.

networks.name: In-House (ravpower.com/pages/affiliate)
networks.commission_rate_notes: Page body confirms 8%-15%. Search meta snippet advertises '8%-20% within 45 days' — 20% likely campaign/incentive tier. No change to base rate since last audit (2026-04-18).
networks.cookie_duration_notes: Page still shows '30/45 DAYS COOKIES'. Commission text body says 'within 30 days of referral click.' Search meta description now prominently says 'within 45 days' — 45-day window appears to be the headline cookie for marketing purposes. Recording 45 as primary.
networks.notes: In-house only — no major affiliate network (Impact, Awin, CJ) detected. Monthly incentive campaigns, bonuses, and special prizes mentioned. Access: product catalog, exclusive promos, banners, text links upon approval. New canonical URL discovered: /pages/affiliate (no typo) now active alongside legacy /pages/afiliates (typo). Both return identical content.

affiliate_program.commission_summary: 8–15% base (potentially up to 20% during campaigns) on DTC sales only. No Amazon channel. In-house program with no managed network layer.
affiliate_program.aov_note: RAVPower advertises $150+ average order value. At 8–15% commission this implies $12–$22.50 per sale at base rate; up to $30 at campaign peak. Higher-AOV positioning (power banks, portable chargers $80–$200 range).

company_status.financial: DISTRESSED-RECOVERY — Amazon ban (June 2021) removed ~31% of parent company (Sunvalleytek) revenue. Pivoted to DTC. Website active as of April 2026. Stock (Sunvalleytek) fell 12% on ban day. Long-term revenue trajectory unclear.
company_status.amazon_ban_reason: Incentivized reviews — offering gift cards in product packaging in exchange for positive Amazon reviews (violation since 2016). Reported by WSJ, confirmed by Amazon.
company_status.positioning: Budget-to-mid range power banks, portable chargers, fast-charging adapters. Direct Amazon competitor profile. Post-ban, DTC focused with affiliate as primary acquisition channel since Amazon Associates route is closed.

competitive_intel.commission_vs_anker: RAVPower 8–15% DTC >> Anker via Amazon Associates ~1–4%. RAVPower's rate is dramatically higher on paper — but Amazon ban means ZERO Amazon affiliate channel. Net reach is far smaller.
competitive_intel.cookie_vs_anker: RAVPower 45-day headline cookie vs Anker Amazon 24-hour cookie. RAVPower wins attribution window by large margin on DTC sales.
competitive_intel.aov_vs_anker: RAVPower claims $150+ AOV. Both brands have moved toward higher-AOV portable power. 8–15% on $150 = $12–$22.50 per sale — meaningful for content affiliates.
competitive_intel.amazon_absence_risk: CRITICAL WEAKNESS — No Amazon presence means no access to the 150M+ Amazon Prime subscriber base for affiliate traffic. Content affiliates who primarily link to Amazon cannot drive RAVPower DTC traffic. Major structural disadvantage vs Anker.
competitive_intel.affiliate_risk: HIGH — Amazon ban history signals compliance/trust risk for affiliates and networks. No major network backing (Impact, Awin) reduces affiliate discoverability. DTC-only program limits affiliate adoption.
competitive_intel.reputation_flag: Review manipulation scandal (2021) affects brand trust in affiliate/creator community. Tech content sites may avoid recommending RAVPower due to historical review inflation.
competitive_intel.opportunity_for_anker: Anker should highlight its legitimate Amazon presence and transparent review history when recruiting affiliates who lost RAVPower programs post-ban. RAVPower's high nominal commission rate is a red herring given reduced reach. Anker's network distribution (Amazon Associates, Impact) vastly outweighs RAVPower's in-house DTC program.
watch_items: Any attempt to rejoin Amazon (brand reinstatement under new seller account)
Launch of third-party network program (Impact, Awin) would signal affiliate investment
Commission rate changes or new incentive structures
Whether 20% campaign rate becomes standardized as base rate

history.event: First audit. RAVPower DTC site confirmed active. In-house affiliate program at 8–15% commission, 30/45-day cookie, $150+ claimed AOV. No managed network (Impact/Awin/CJ) detected. Amazon ban still in effect since June 2021 — no evidence of reinstatement. High commission rate obscured by severely limited distribution vs Anker's multi-channel footprint.

Second audit. Program terms unchanged: 8–15% commission, 30/45-day cookie, $150+ AOV, in-house only. Two notable observations: (1) New canonical URL /pages/affiliate discovered (no typo) — both pages serve identical content. (2) Search meta snippet now prominently advertises '8%-20% within 45 days' — 20% is likely campaign rate; page body still shows 8%-15%. No Amazon reinstatement detected. No new network launch. No material change — no alert fired.
